name: Razorpay Resilience Chaos Suite
scenario: Razorpay's circuit breakers look correct in code review but a recent production incident showed the HDFC CB didn't open during a DNS failure. Build a chaos engineering suite to validate resilience mechanisms before they're needed in production.

core.description: SteadyState record (p99Ms, errorRatePct, cbState). SteadyStateMonitor @Service: captureBaseline() returns normal metrics, isInSteadyState(ss) validates p99 < 500 AND error < 1% AND cbState = CLOSED.
ChaosHypothesis record (maxP99Ms, maxErrorPct, requireCbOpen). isMet(metrics, fallbackCount) → true only if ALL 3 conditions satisfied.
PaymentService @Service with @CircuitBreaker on HDFC call. Fallback method: returns PaymentResponse with status=FALLBACK. Chaos toggle: enableChaos() throws RuntimeException on every call.
ChaosExperimentRunner @Service: run(experiment, chaosEnabled) → ChaosOutcome. Records: CB state, fallback count, whether hypothesis was met.
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): normal op has 0 fallbacks, chaos activates fallback, hypothesis met when CB opens, hypothesis violated when no fallback. All 4 pass.
README: explains why testing only with 'no exception thrown' misses resilience bugs (with the HDFC DNS failure example).
Recovery test: after chaos disabled, service returns to normal behavior (fallback NOT called).

bonus.description: GameDayExperiment records for 3 scenarios: HDFC latency, payment DB exception, notification service down. Each with hypothesis, blast radius, and rollback procedure. isValid() validation.
ChaosGapTracker @Service: recordGap(DiscoveredGap), markFixed(experimentName), getOpenGaps(). Tests: gap tracked, fix applied, open gaps filtered correctly.
Database chaos test: DatabaseChaosSimulator throwing TransientDataAccessException → PaymentService returns cached payment list → assert cached data AND fallback counter > 0.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Three required assertions for chaos tests: (1) CircuitBreaker state = OPEN or HALF_OPEN (not CLOSED), (2) fallback counter > 0 (fallback actually ran), (3) p99 stayed within threshold (fallback was fast). All three needed — just 'no exception' is not enough. Chaos toggle: simplest implementation is a boolean flag (chaosEnabled=true → throw RuntimeException). @CircuitBreaker fallback method: must have same signature as main method PLUS the exception type as last parameter. Reset between tests: @BeforeEach must disable chaos and reset fallback counter (AtomicInteger.set(0)). Steady state first: always capture baseline metrics before injecting chaos — compare baseline vs chaos metrics in assertions.
